bead.title: catalog-cache: dispatch failures must invalidate UnreachableAt; today only probes do
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Fizeau routes to local LMStudio/MLX endpoints that are demonstrably dead at dispatch time, burning 5-second i/o timeouts per dispatch and pinning sticky leases to dead hosts. Symptom from this morning: bragi-club-3090 / vidar / grendel all timed out on direct TCP probes at the time of investigation, yet Fizeau routed to bragi-club-3090 with `source_status: "available"` and `eligible: true` in the routing_decision event.

ROOT CAUSE
The catalog cache (service_catalog_cache.go) treats GET /v1/models probe failures as the SOLE signal source for the UnreachableAt timestamp. Chat-completions dispatch failures (POST /v1/chat/completions returning `dial tcp ...: i/o timeout`, `connection refused`, etc.) are NOT fed back into the cache. The next routing pass within FreshTTL (60s) re-reads the same stale "available" entry and re-selects the dead endpoint.

Concrete code path:
- service_catalog_cache.go:80 — `UnreachableAt time.Time // zero unless last attempt produced a ReachabilityError`
- service_catalog_cache.go:295-307 — the ONLY writes to UnreachableAt are inside `probe()`, set when probeFn returns `*openai.ReachabilityError`.
- service_probe.go:164-184 — probeOpenAIModels is the probeFn; it classifies GET /v1/models failures into ReachabilityError. Chat-completions failures never reach this function.
- Dispatch code in service_execute.go / the harness HTTP path returns transport errors directly to the caller. No code path calls `cache.MarkUnreachable(endpoint)` (no such API exists) or otherwise touches `e.UnreachableAt` from the dispatch flow.

Compound the cache behavior with two further amplifiers:
1. **Per-CLI-invocation cache isolation.** Each `ddx work` (and each `ddx run`) spawns its own fizeau service in-process; the catalog cache is local heap. ddx-on-project-A's worker may know bragi is unreachable; ddx-on-project-B's worker has an empty cache and probes bragi successfully once (at startup), then trusts that result for FreshTTL (60s) + StaleTTL (10min stale-while-revalidate).
2. **Stale-while-revalidate widens the false-positive window.** service_catalog_cache.go:240 returns the stale-but-cached "available" entry and kicks an async refresh. Synchronous callers receive `available` without waiting for the refresh result.

EVIDENCE FROM DOWNSTREAM DDX
Routing decision for the failed dispatch (.ddx/executions/20260516T174611-788fbe14/embedded/svc-*.jsonl):

  candidate: bragi-club-3090
  source_status: "available"
  eligible: true
  score: 35
  model_discovery_freshness_at: 2026-05-16T17:46:14.952Z

Session.end event 7 seconds later:

  status: error
  error: "agent: provider error: openai: Post \"http://bragi:8020/v1/chat/completions\": dial tcp 100.127.38.115:8020: i/o timeout"
  duration_ms: 5027

Direct TCP test from the same host at the time of investigation: `timeout 5 bash -c 'echo > /dev/tcp/bragi/8020'` failed. Same result on grendel:* and vidar:* (all dead). Yet fizeau's auto-route enumerated bragi/grendel/vidar as `source_status: "available"` because the catalog cache had not been told they were unreachable — only chat-completions had observed the timeout, and the chat-completions path does not write back to the cache.

A separate ddx routing-decision from 24 minutes earlier (different project, same hosts, different fizeau service instance) DID correctly mark bragi-club-3090 as `eligible: false, filter_reason: "endpoint_unreachable"` — but that instance had freshly probed and the probe had failed. The per-instance cache isolation means the "dead" knowledge does not propagate to other service instances.

PROPOSED FIX

1. **Add a dispatch-feedback hook on the catalog cache.** Add a method `cache.RecordDispatchError(endpoint, err error)` (or similar). The dispatch code path in service_execute.go (and any other place where chat-completions is invoked through an OpenAI-compatible adapter) calls this after a failed request. RecordDispatchError classifies err via the same `isNetworkFailure` / `isServerError` predicates that service_probe.go:188-223 already uses; if classified as ReachabilityError-equivalent, set `e.UnreachableAt = time.Now()` and `e.LastErr = err`. Honor the same UnreachableCooldown semantics so the next route pass excludes the endpoint.

2. **Tighten FreshTTL for local subprocess endpoints.** 60s is reasonable for stable cloud APIs but too lenient for laptop-class local servers that suspend/resume (LMStudio on a sleeping MacBook). Either make FreshTTL configurable per provider class, or default it to 10s for `deployment_class: local` and keep 60s for managed_cloud_*.

3. **Make UnreachableCooldown start counting from MOST RECENT failure, not first.** Today UnreachableAt is set once on probe failure and cleared on next success. If dispatch failures repeatedly recur but no probe runs in between, UnreachableAt may be stale. (3) is dependent on (1); without (1), only probes write UnreachableAt so this is moot.

4. **(Optional, larger)** Persist UnreachableAt across service instances via a small on-disk cache file (`~/.cache/fizeau/route-health.json` or similar) with TTL. So `ddx work` invocations across projects share aliveness knowledge, eliminating the per-process false-positive window. NON-SCOPE for this bead; track as follow-up if (1)+(2) prove insufficient.

NON-SCOPE
- Sticky lease vs aliveness interaction (separate bead worth filing; today's evidence is that sticky DID NOT override aliveness in this case — the bug is upstream of sticky in the cache).
- DDX-side changes; ddx passes policy=default verbatim per CONTRACT-003. Fix is entirely Fizeau-side.
- Building the persistent cross-process cache (option 4 above).

CROSS-REF
- fizeau-80d4bf41 (auto-route candidate enumeration collapses multi-tier subscription harnesses to DefaultModel only) — same symptom surface, different root cause.
- fizeau-0bc93861 (openrouter: drop from candidate pool when credential or credit is missing) — same pattern (provider health gating), different provider class.
- Github issue mirror: file as a follow-up to https://github.com/easel/fizeau/issues/6 once this is filed.
bead.acceptance:
1. New exported method on the catalog cache: `RecordDispatchError(endpoint string, err error)` (or equivalent name). Called from every chat-completions code path after a failed dispatch. Classifies err using the same isNetworkFailure/isServerError predicates as probeOpenAIModels; on classification as ReachabilityError-equivalent, sets `e.UnreachableAt = now` and `e.LastErr = err`.
2. TestCatalogCacheRecordsDispatchFailureAsUnreachable — given a successful probe at T0 followed by a chat-completions dispatch failure at T1 with `dial tcp ... i/o timeout`, the cache entry has `UnreachableAt = T1` after the dispatch returns. Verified by direct cache-state inspection.
3. TestRoutePicksAlternateAfterDispatchFailureFeedback — given two equally-scored endpoints A and B, both initially `available` in cache. First dispatch picks A (sticky lease acquired) and fails with i/o timeout. Second dispatch in same service instance: routing_decision shows A as `eligible: false, filter_reason: "endpoint_unreachable"`, and B is selected.
4. TestNoSilentRecoveryFromUnreachable — after dispatch-failure marks an endpoint unreachable, the next routing pass within UnreachableCooldown does NOT re-select it even if the cache's stale entry is otherwise within StaleTTL.
5. FreshTTL for `deployment_class: local` providers is ≤ 15s by default (configurable). Verified by a unit test that constructs a local-class catalog entry and asserts the resolved FreshTTL.
6. Existing probe-driven path remains unchanged: probe failures continue to set UnreachableAt as today; probe successes still clear it on next probe.
7. No regression in service_routing tests, service_probe tests, or service_adr005_test.go.
8. cd /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau && go test ./... green.
9. lefthook run pre-commit green.
